rulings.Case Title: MARTHA JOAN HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Special Electric Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JODIE D. COLLINS SR. et al VS. CRANE CO. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 31, 2014 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.'s general demurrer to plaintiffs' first amended complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part. The one-year statute of limitations under C.C.P. 340.2 has not run as Mr. Collins has suffered no disability within the meaning of that section. (See Hamilton v. Asbestos Corp. Ltd. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127.) The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to defendant's C.C.P. 474 argument. Plaintiff's 2009 action, of which this Court takes judicial notice, demonstrates that plaintiffs were not ignorant of defendant's name at the time they filed this action and thus plaintiffs are precluded from invoking the procedure set forth in C.C.P. 474. Since the statute of limitations has not run, plaintiffs have 10 days leave to amend to add Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc. as a defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
